title: Title
notes:
Open with the system identity, not the model details.

Say this is an AI-assisted decision-support prototype for wind turbine operations.

Emphasise the three-part thesis: diagnosis, explanation, and advisory optimisation.

Most important line: the AI does not directly control a real turbine.

title: Problem
notes:
Use this slide to create the engineering pain point.

Do not say 'AI can solve everything'. Say the real gap is between fault detection and operator action.

Frame the project as decision support on top of existing SCADA, not a hardware replacement.

title: System Overview
notes:
Explain the three coordinated flows clearly.

Main flow: SCADA to operator briefing.

RL is simulation advisory, and operators remain final decision-makers.

title: Pipeline
notes:
Use the clickable stages if you want to slow down the explanation.

Walk left to right on the main flow first. Then mention RL comes in from the side as advisory input.

Do not call it model fusion. Call it a governance chain.

title: Metadata
notes:
This is the white-box slide.

Say that downstream modules do not consume opaque latent vectors. They consume interpretable turbine-day indicators.

This is the strongest explanation slide for your explainable-AI point.

title: RL Advisory Module
notes:
Keep RL grounded in simulation.

Explain multi-objective balancing: power capture, load reduction, and smoothness.

Reinforce advisory-only constraint.

title: Case Study
notes:
Show three turbine-day cases with different health states.

Emphasize decision guidance, not just anomaly detection.

Tie each case to control posture.

title: Explainable & Controllable AI
notes:
This slide defines why the AI behavior is interpretable and controllable.

Explain metadata, explicit safety rules, and transparent objectives.

Reinforce human final authority.

title: Engineering Value
notes:
Highlight value across operations, maintenance, sustainability, and carbon impact.

Stress that novelty is system-level integration, not only model score.

title: Deployment Path
notes:
Move from demo to roadmap.

State current prototype capabilities and future development directions.

End with scalable long-term vision.

title: Live Demo
notes:
Transition from Theory to Practice.

Show the seamless flow from SCADA to AI advisory report.

Engage the audience to observe the live speed and reasoning of the model.

title: Conclusion
notes:
Finish with the responsible-AI message.

Repeat the safety principle: advisory only, not autonomous control.

Pause after the final takeaway sentence.

title: Thank You
notes:
Thank the audience.

Invite questions and discussion.

Keep it brief.
